# Tasks — EEx Code Block Support in Cheex Templates
*Generated by create_tasks on 2026-03-19*

## Objective
Enable `<% ... %>` code blocks in Cheex templates so that variables defined in code blocks are available to subsequent expressions at the same scope level, using binding threading via quoted AST generation.

## Scope
- **In Scope**: Parser `:code_block` AST nodes, compiler `__code_block__` markers, `compiled_to_quoted` binding threading with `Enum.map_reduce`, `map_contains_code_block?` detection, code blocks at top level/nested/for-loop bodies, error handling with line/column, passthrough in `resolve_partials` and `validate_platform!`, tests at all layers
- **Out of Scope**: Code blocks affecting parent scope, compile-time code block evaluation, partial `substitute_bindings` support for code blocks, multi-line code blocks spanning multiple tags

## Requirements Traced
| ID | Description | Source | Tasks |
|----|-------------|--------|-------|
| REQ-001 | Code blocks define variables available to subsequent sibling elements | plan.md | 1.1, 2.1, 3.1, 4.1 |
| REQ-002 | Code blocks produce no output in the data structure | plan.md | 3.1 |
| REQ-003 | Code blocks work inside for-loop bodies (per-iteration scope) | plan.md | 1.1, 3.1, 4.1 |
| REQ-004 | Error messages include source line/column on code block failure | plan.md | 3.1 |
| REQ-005 | Existing for-loop and EEx behavior unchanged | implicit | 4.1 |

---

## Architecture Context

### Where This Fits
- Extends the Cheex template pipeline at 3 layers: parser (`lib/juvet/template/parser.ex`), compiler (`lib/juvet/template/compiler/slack.ex`), and template code generation (`lib/juvet/template.ex`)
- Follows the exact pattern established by for-loop support: AST node → compiled marker → quoted AST generation
- Tokenizer already produces `:eex_code` tokens — no changes needed there

### Technical Approach
- **Pattern**: Extend for-loop's `compiled_to_quoted` approach with `Enum.map_reduce` for binding threading
- **Why**: Maintains Cheex's compile-time-first philosophy while enabling runtime variable scoping
- **Leveraging**: `map_contains_for?`, `compiled_to_quoted`, `chunk_to_quoted_segment` as reference implementations

### Key Decisions
- Code blocks only affect subsequent siblings at the same list level (matches EEx scoping semantics)
- Runtime execution via `Code.eval_string(code, bindings)` which returns `{result, updated_bindings}` — naturally threads variables forward
- Code block markers filtered as `nil` from output lists

---

## Tasks

### Phase 1: Parser — Code Block AST Nodes

#### [1.1] Create `:code_block` AST nodes from `:eex_code` tokens
- [ ] **1.1.1** Modify top-level `do_parse` to create `:code_block` nodes for non-`"end"` `:eex_code` tokens in `lib/juvet/template/parser.ex`
  - **Produces**: `%{node_type: :code_block, code: String.t(), line: integer, column: integer}` AST nodes in parsed output
  - **Consumed by**: `compile_element` in `compiler/slack.ex` (task 2.1)
  - **Replaces**: Skip clause at line 72 that silently discards all `:eex_code` tokens
  - [ ] `:eex_code` tokens with content `"end"` are still skipped (existing behavior preserved)
  - [ ] All other `:eex_code` tokens produce `:code_block` AST nodes with `code`, `line`, and `column` fields
  - [ ] Code block nodes appear in correct order relative to sibling element nodes

- [ ] **1.1.2** Add the same `:code_block` creation to `nested_elements` and `parse_for_body` in `lib/juvet/template/parser.ex`
  - **Produces**: `:code_block` nodes in nested element children and for-loop body lists
  - **Consumed by**: Same compiler path as 1.1.1
  - **Replaces**: Skip clause at line 291 (`nested_elements`) that discards `:eex_code` tokens
  - [ ] `nested_elements` creates `:code_block` nodes for non-`"end"` `:eex_code` tokens (replaces skip at line 291)
  - [ ] `parse_for_body` creates `:code_block` nodes for non-`"end"` `:eex_code` tokens (existing `"end"` handling at line 381 preserved)

- [ ] **1.1.3** Update `resolve_partials` and `validate_platform!` to pass through `:code_block` nodes in `lib/juvet/template.ex`
  - **Produces**: `:code_block` nodes survive partial resolution and platform validation unchanged
  - **Consumed by**: Compiler (task 2.1) receives complete AST with code blocks intact
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new passthrough, mirrors existing `:for_loop` handling)
  - [ ] `resolve_partials` handles `%{node_type: :code_block}` by passing it through unchanged (same pattern as `:for_loop` at the existing clause)
  - [ ] `validate_platform!` handles `%{node_type: :code_block}` as a no-op (code blocks have no platform)

### Phase 2: Compiler — Code Block Markers

#### [2.1] Add `compile_element` clause for `:code_block` nodes
- [ ] **2.1.1** Add `:code_block` compilation to `lib/juvet/template/compiler/slack.ex`
  - **Produces**: `%{__code_block__: true, code: String.t(), line: integer, column: integer}` compiled map markers
  - **Consumed by**: `map_contains_code_block?` detection and `compiled_to_quoted` in `template.ex` (task 3.1)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new clause, follows `__for__: true` pattern at line 69-76)
  - [ ] `compile_element(%{node_type: :code_block})` returns map with `__code_block__: true`, preserving `code`, `line`, and `column`
  - [ ] Clause is added before the catch-all element clause so it matches correctly

### Phase 3: Template — Detection and Code Generation

#### [3.1] Add code block detection and binding-threading AST generation to `lib/juvet/template.ex`
- [ ] **3.1.1** Add `map_contains_code_block?/1` detection function
  - **Produces**: Boolean indicating whether compiled structure contains `__code_block__` markers
  - **Consumed by**: `generate_map_function` and `generate_json_function` routing conditions (task 3.1.2)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new function, mirrors `map_contains_for?` at lines 810-819)
  - [ ] Recursively scans maps, lists, and detects `%{__code_block__: true}` markers
  - [ ] Returns `false` for all other values (strings, atoms, numbers, etc.)

- [ ] **3.1.2** Modify function generation routing to include code block detection
  - **Produces**: Templates with code blocks route to the quoted AST code path (same as for-loops)
  - **Consumed by**: `compiled_to_quoted` which generates the runtime code (task 3.1.3)
  - **Replaces**: N/A (extends existing condition at lines 587/618)
  - [ ] `generate_map_function` condition becomes `map_contains_for?(compiled) or map_contains_code_block?(compiled)`
  - [ ] `generate_json_function` condition updated identically
  - [ ] Existing for-loop and EEx-only paths continue to work unchanged

- [ ] **3.1.3** Implement binding-threading `compiled_to_quoted` for lists containing code blocks
  - **Produces**: Quoted Elixir AST that uses `Enum.map_reduce` to thread bindings through element sequences, executing code blocks via `Code.eval_string` and passing updated bindings to subsequent `eval_map` calls
  - **Consumed by**: Generated template functions at runtime
  - **Replaces**: N/A (extends `compiled_to_quoted` list clause at lines 696-709)
  - [ ] When a list contains `__code_block__` markers, generates `Enum.map_reduce` that: (a) code blocks execute via `Code.eval_string(code, bindings)` returning `{nil, updated_bindings}`, (b) for-loop elements evaluate with current bindings, (c) regular elements evaluate via `eval_map(element, bindings)` returning `{result, same_bindings}`
  - [ ] `nil` values from code blocks are filtered out of the final list
  - [ ] Lists with BOTH code blocks and for-loops work correctly (map_reduce handles both)

- [ ] **3.1.4** Add `compiled_to_quoted` clause for `%{__code_block__: true}` maps
  - **Produces**: Quoted AST for individual code block execution with error wrapping
  - **Consumed by**: The `map_reduce` callback generated in task 3.1.3
  - **Replaces**: N/A (new clause, before the generic `when is_map(map)` clause at line 687)
  - [ ] Generates `Code.eval_string(code, bindings)` call for the code block's code string
  - [ ] Wraps execution with error handling that re-raises with template line/column context from the `__code_block__` marker
  - [ ] Error messages include "Error in code block at line X, column Y: <original message>"

### Phase 4: Testing

#### [4.1] Add tests at all layers
- [ ] **4.1.1** Add parser tests for code block AST nodes in `test/juvet/template/parser_test.exs`
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for parser code block behavior
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test: `<% x = 1 %>` at top level produces `%{node_type: :code_block, code: "x = 1"}` AST node
  - [ ] Test: Code block inside `blocks:` children produces `:code_block` node in children list
  - [ ] Test: Code block inside for-loop body produces `:code_block` node in body list
  - [ ] Test: `<% end %>` is still handled as for-loop terminator (not a code block)
  - [ ] Test: Multiple code blocks interleaved with elements preserve correct order

- [ ] **4.1.2** Add compiler tests for code block marking in `test/juvet/template/compiler/slack_test.exs`
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for compiler code block transformation
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test: `:code_block` AST node compiles to `%{__code_block__: true, code: ..., line: ..., column: ...}`
  - [ ] Test: Code blocks in a blocks list compile alongside regular elements correctly

- [ ] **4.1.3** Add end-to-end template tests in `test/juvet/template_test.exs`
  - **Produces**: Test coverage for full pipeline with code blocks
  - **Consumed by**: CI/test suite
  - **Replaces**: N/A
  - [ ] Test: Template with `<% x = 1 %>` followed by `.section{text: "<%= x %>"}` renders the variable value
  - [ ] Test: Multiple sequential code blocks — second block can reference first block's variable
  - [ ] Test: Code block inside for-loop body — variable scoped per iteration
  - [ ] Test: Code block with function call (e.g., `<% result = String.upcase(name) %>`) works with bindings
  - [ ] Test: Error in code block raises with line/column context
  - [ ] Test: Existing for-loop templates still work (regression check)
  - [ ] Test: Existing EEx-only templates still work (regression check)

## Execution Strategies

### Sequential Execution
1. Task 1.1 - Parser code block AST nodes (no dependencies)
2. Task 2.1 - Compiler code block markers (depends on 1.1)
3. Task 3.1 - Template detection and code generation (depends on 2.1)
4. Task 4.1 - Tests at all layers (depends on 1.1, 2.1, 3.1)

### Parallel Execution

**Wave 1**: 1.1
- Rationale: Parser changes are the foundation; compiler and template depend on AST node structure

**Wave 2 (after Wave 1)**: 2.1
- Rationale: Compiler needs parser AST nodes to compile

**Wave 3 (after Wave 2)**: 3.1
- Rationale: Template code generation needs compiled markers to detect and transform

**Wave 4 (after Wave 3)**: 4.1
- Rationale: Tests verify the complete pipeline
